video:video.video:title: China’s manufacturers are under pressure | FT Transact
video:video.video:description: China has long been regarded as the factory of the world, and accounts for over 30 per cent of the world’s value-added manufacturing. But, as the FT’s William Langley explains, factors ranging from demographics to competition from rival hubs are all putting the squeeze on China’s factory output, and finding solutions to those challenges may not be easy

video:video.video:title: What impact is the Digital Markets Act having? | FT Tech
video:video.video:description: The EU is trying to curb tech leaders' dominance of the digital marketplace with the Digital Markets Act, which it began enforcing in March. The aim is to improve consumer choice and open up markets for European start-ups to flourish. But, as the FT’s Javier Espinoza explains, while the legislation is already having an impact, Big Tech shouldn’t be underestimated

video:video.video:title: Can shale protect America from oil shocks? | FT Energy Source
video:video.video:description: For decades the US has been susceptible to oil price shocks due to political volatility in the Middle East. However, as the FT’s Jamie Smyth explains, the landscape has been transformed by the US shale revolution. With global markets now awash with American oil, are price surges a thing of the past for the US?

video:video.video:title: How to retain your workforce | FT Working It
